clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for ed asthma exacerbation documentation.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Emergency Medicine documentation-only documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct high-caution documentation workflow for clinician-stated assessment and plans around ed asthma exacerbation documentation, without generating management advice.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
red_flag_prompts: time-critical symptoms or abnormal observations documented if assessed
injury mechanism and safeguarding concern documented if relevant
escalation or disposition documented only if clinician decided

chips.text: ED asthma exacerbation documentation context documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
chips.evidence_relationship: The exact DHA asthma symptoms section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context for a suspected exacerbation without inferring severity class or diagnosis.

history_draft.text: Patient presents for ed asthma exacerbation documentation documentation. [Onset/duration]. [Symptom context]. [Associated symptoms reviewed]. [Relevant negatives]. [Patient concerns/goals]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Vital signs documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Focused examination documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Injury/illness severity documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. General appearance documented.

clinical_blockers: Prior diagnosis and baseline control, trigger, medication use and response, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, peak flow, auscultation, severity classification, treatment, dose, reassessment, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
